title: Journal of the Whills, Part I
content:
{{Top|rwm|ncl|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|Whills}} {{Canceled|no=1}} {{Book |image= |title=''Journal of the Whills, Part I'' |author=[[George Lucas]]''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]'' |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher= |early release= |release date=Canceled |intl release= |rereleased= |media type= |pages= |isbn= |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline= |series= |preceded by= |followed by=[[The Star Wars story synopsis|''The Star Wars'' story synopsis]] }} The '''''Journal of the Whills, [Part] I''''',''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]'' or '''''Journal of the Whills, Part I''''' was a handwritten two-page partial outline for the story that would, after many further rewrites and explorations of ideas, become ''[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Star Wars]]'' ([[1977]]). It was written by [[George Lucas]], starting around [[January]] [[1973]].''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]'', ''[[The Star Wars Archives: Episodes IV–VI, 1977–1983]]'', and {{BookCite|author=Kaminski, Michael|title=The Secret History of Star Wars|date=2007|publisher=Legacy Books Press}} support a [[January]] [[1973]] date for ''Journal of the Whills'', while ''[[The Cinema of George Lucas]]'' incorrectly dates the outline to [[1972]]. ==Plot summary== The outline begins: "This is the story of Mace Windy, a revered Jedi-Bendu of Ophuchi, as related to us by C.J. Thorpe, padawaan learner to the famed Jedi." Thorpe, called C.J. or C.2. (it switches back and forth in the document), stands for "Chuiee Two Thorpe of Kissel. My father is Han Dardell Thorpe, chief pilot of the renown galactic cruiser Tarnack." When Chuiee reaches 16, he enters the Intersystems Academy to train as a potential Jedi-Templer. There, he becomes padawaan learner to Mace Windy, a Warlord to the Chairman of the Alliance of Independent Systems. Windy becomes the victim of a court conspiracy: those who fear that Windy is more powerful than the Imperial leader of the Galactic Empire, some of whom are his comrades, arrange for his replacement and expulsion from the royal forces. Though Windy has been dismissed, Chuiee begs to stay in his service. This leads to the even more fragmentary story of Journal of the Whills, Part II. After 4 years, Windy and Chuiee have their greatest adventure. They guard a shipment of fusion portables to Yavin, where they are summoned to the desolate planet Yoshiro by a mysterious courier sent by the Alliance chairman. The narrative drops off at this point. ==Inspiration== The outline draws inspiration from the first chapter of the the 1931 John Carter novel ''A Fighting Man of Mars'' by Edgar Rice Burroughs. For example, the following passage from Burroughs' first chapter parallels Lucas' outline:
Never shall I forget the occasion upon which I first laid eyes upon Sanoma Tora. It was upon the occasion of a great feast at the marble palace of The Warlord. There were gathered under one roof the most beautiful women of Barsoomhttps://www.gutenberg.org/cache/epub/70691/pg70691.txt
Never shall I forget the occasion upon which I first set eyes upon Mace Windy. It was at the great feast of the Pleabs. There were gathered under one roof, the most powerful warriors in the Galaxyhttps://medium.com/@Oozer3993/everything-known-about-the-journal-of-the-whills-outline-d8db1f1410ea
==Legacy== When [[George Lucas]] began work on [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I]], he took the ''Journal of the Whills'' out of the [[Lucasfilm]] archives, and reread it. It sat on his desk as he wrote the first handwritten script for the first [[Prequel trilogy|prequel]].''[[The Cinema of George Lucas]]'' ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: The Annotated Screenplays]]'' *''[[The Cinema of George Lucas]]'' *{{InsiderCite|92|Unknown Origins}} *''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]'' *''[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle]]'' *[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle (updated edition)|''Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle'', Updated Edition]] *''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, Updated and Expanded Edition]]'' *''[[The Star Wars Archives: Episodes IV–VI, 1977–1983]]'' *''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |ka=უილების დღიური, ნაწილი I }} [[Category:Script drafts|4-01]]
